2014-05-16 acq. '''[[Nagne]]''' }}{{TOCRWI}} '''KT Rolster''' is a Korean multi-gaming organization originally founded in 1999 under the name KTF MagicNs. The team eventually changed to the name KT Rolster in August 2009. Along with their two League of Legends teams, [[KT Rolster Arrows]] and '''KT Rolster Bullets''', they also sponsor a well-known StarCraft II team. == History == == Timeline == {{TeamNews}} == Player Roster == [[File:KT Bullets 2014 OGN Summer.jpg|thumb|no-link=true|350px|right|KT Rolster Bullets OGN Summer 2014 Lineup]] {{TeamMembersCurrent}} ===Former=== {{TeamMembersFormer}} == Player League Participation == {{TeamPlayerLeagueHistory}} ==Organization== ===Former=== {{listplayer/Start|staff=yes|newteam=yes}} {{listplayer|FIFAHUN|kr|Lee Ji-hoon (이지훈)|'''Head Coach'''|newteam=KT}} {{listplayer|ZanDarc|kr|Oh Chang-jong (오창종)|'''Coach'''|newteam=KT}} {{listplayersp||kr|Kim Hwan (김환)|'''Coach'''|newteam=KT}} {{listplayer|VicaL|kr|Kim Sun-mook (김선묵)|'''Coach'''|newteam=SH Royal}} {{listplayer/End}} == Tournaments == {{TeamResults|show=overviewpage}} {{TeamShowmatchResults|show=overviewpage}} ===As KT Rolster B=== {{TeamResults|KT Rolster B|show=overviewpage}} == Highlight Videos == ==Interviews== === 2013 === * March 16, [http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gJktoU6xYWU KT Rolster B talks about playing Curse and their time in the USA (video)] ''with GameSpot'' ==Articles== ===2014=== * November 17, [http://na.lolesports.com/articles/kt-roller-coaster-ride A KT roller coaster ride] ''from LoL Esports'' ==Links== ==References==
